metallogenic_belt: Pine Creek inlier, northern Australia
tectonic_setting: Contact metamorphic aureole of Proterozoic I-type granite, within a fold and thrust belt

source.evidence: Magmatic brine from crystallizing I-type granite, with high F and Cl; metamorphic fluid from devolatilizing metasediments

transport.evidence: Upward fluid flow focused into bedding-parallel fractures; near-lithostatic pressure gradient

trap.evidence: Fluid mixing with reduced carbonic fluid from carbonaceous slate; reduction triggers gold precipitation

reservoir.evidence: Quartz veins in bedding-parallel fractures within hornfels; crack-seal textures

seal.evidence: Carbonaceous slate acts as a redox barrier; no explicit seal mentioned

timing.evidence: Peak contact metamorphism at 550-620°C, 200 MPa; granite intrusion at 1835-1818 Ma

preservation.evidence: Post-mineralization deformation and thrusting; some preservation of veins

metallogenic_belt: Fore-Sudetic monocline, Lubin copper mining district, southwestern Poland
tectonic_setting: Fore-Sudetic monocline, Permian Zechstein basin, passive margin setting

source.evidence: Metals sourced from ascending oxidizing fluids; copper leaching by saline waters

transport.evidence: Fluid transport via fractures and permeable horizons; diffusion/advection of H2S

trap.evidence: Chemical trap due to redox gradients; organic-rich shales and sulfate reduction

reservoir.evidence: Host rocks include sandstone, dolomite, and mudstone with porosity and permeability

seal.evidence: Impermeable horizons (e.g., clay-rich Kupferschiefer) act as seals

timing.evidence: Timing debated: early diagenesis vs. late diagenesis; low-temperature anilite suggests early

preservation.evidence: Preserved in mines; some alteration by recent mine waters

metallogenic_belt: northern Coast Ranges, California
tectonic_setting: fore-arc basin, strike-slip and fold-thrust belt, associated with Clear Lake volcanic field

source.evidence: Lead isotopes indicate crustal source, possibly Great Valley sequence or mixture of radiogenic and nonradiogenic sources; sulfur isotopes suggest magmatic sulfur

transport.evidence: Meteoric water circulated through sedimentary rocks, evolved to high δ18O; fluids migrated along Stony Creek fault and dilation zones

trap.evidence: Boiling and phase separation in near-surface environment; steep thermal and pressure gradients; hydrothermal eruption breccias

reservoir.evidence: Sheeted vein complex in dilation zone at rheologic contrast between competent basalt and incompetent melange

seal.evidence: Self-sealing by silica precipitation; sinter cap preserved

timing.evidence: K-Ar ages of alunite 0.75-2.2 Ma; maximum age from basaltic andesite 2.2 ± 0.2 Ma

preservation.evidence: Subaerial sinter preserved, indicating minimal post-mineralization erosion

metallogenic_belt: Cordilleran magmatic arc, Great Basin
tectonic_setting: Extensional to transtensional intra-arc graben within the early Mesozoic Cordilleran magmatic arc

source.evidence: Stable isotope data indicate dominantly felsic igneous source for sulfur; high K calc-alkaline dacite porphyry swarm genetically linked

transport.evidence: Fluids transported along permeable structures: Pickhandle thrust and lower Candelaria shear, enhanced by brecciation and delamination of host shales

trap.evidence: Ore precipitated in tabular zones along thrust and shear due to chemical (carbonaceous marly shales) and structural (permeable breccia zones) traps

reservoir.evidence: Host rocks include reactive carbonaceous marly shales (member 1 of Candelaria Formation) and mafic-ultramafic units; ore zones up to 30 m thick

seal.evidence: Overlying Pickhandle allochthon and less permeable units may have acted as seals; post-mineralization thrusts and later Basin-Range faulting affected preservation

timing.evidence: U-Pb zircon age of porphyry dikes at 192 ± 3 Ma; mineralization is syn- to late-magmatic, estimated between 187 and 194 Ma

preservation.evidence: Supergene oxidation to 200 m depth; K-Ar sericite ages (91-129 Ma) indicate partial resetting by Cretaceous thermal event, but deposit largely preserved

metallogenic_belt: Iron Springs mining district, southwestern Utah, Basin and Range Province
tectonic_setting: Miocene laccoliths emplaced in a continental arc setting, related to Sevier thrust faults

source.evidence: Iron derived from subsolidus alteration of quartz monzonite, with mass balance indicating sufficient Fe from selvage alteration of 40 km3 of intrusive rock.

transport.evidence: Fluid transported via selvage joints and fractures; Cl-bearing aqueous fluid with FeCl complexes; evidence from vein mineral assemblages and fluid inclusions.

trap.evidence: Strata-bound orebodies in Homestake Limestone; replacement at constant volume; precipitation due to cooling and possibly fluid-rock reaction.

reservoir.evidence: Limestone host provides reactive chemical trap; porosity about 20% in ore; volume-for-volume replacement.

seal.evidence: Peripheral shell and overlying impermeable sedimentary rocks (Temple Cap Sandstone, Carmel Formation) acted as seals; selvage joints did not penetrate peripheral shell.

timing.evidence: K-Ar ages of biotite from laccoliths: 21.5-22.6 Ma; ore formation postdates solidus, with calcite-dolomite temperature of 485°C.

preservation.evidence: Orebodies preserved at surface; some erosion has occurred; Stoddard Mountain pluton lacks mineralization due to different wall rock and shallower emplacement.

structural_controls: flow-banded and flow-brecciated portions of flows
metallogenic_belt: Bushveld Complex, Kaapvaal Craton
tectonic_setting: Intracontinental large igneous province, associated with the 2.06 Ga Bushveld magmatic event

source.evidence: Metals (Pb, Zn, Mn) derived from volcanic rocks and possibly from fluids originating from floor rocks and xenoliths within the Rustenburg Layered Suite.

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal fluids migrated through permeable pyroclastic horizons and flow margins, driven by heat from the Rustenburg Layered Suite.

trap.evidence: Precipitation of sulfides (pyrite, arsenopyrite) and enrichment of Pb, Zn, Mn in hydrated, altered roof rocks, particularly in porous zones.

reservoir.evidence: Roof rocks of the Rooiberg Group, especially felsites and pyroclastic units, provided permeable pathways and reactive host rocks.

seal.evidence: Overlying less altered volcanic rocks may have acted as a seal, but no specific seal is described.

timing.evidence: Two stages: early pyrite-arsenopyrite mineralization possibly related to granophyre intrusion; later Pb-Zn-Mn enrichment linked to emplacement of the Rustenburg Layered Suite at ~2.06 Ga.

preservation.evidence: The roof rocks are preserved in the Bushveld Complex roof, with only low-grade metamorphism, indicating good preservation despite later deformation.

metallogenic_belt: Cascade Mountains, southern Coast Plutonic Complex
tectonic_setting: Tectonic contact between Permian-Jurassic ocean floor/island-arc rocks (Hozameen Group) and Jurassic turbiditic sediments (Ladner Group), near Coquihalla serpentine belt

source.evidence: Highly evolved meteoric waters as ore-forming fluids; metals likely derived from host rocks or deeper sources

transport.evidence: Fluid flow along permeable coarse clastic zones and structural conduits (F2 fold hinge, faults)

trap.evidence: Saddle reef morphology in tectonically thickened hinge zone of F2 antiform; chemical precipitation due to temperature decrease (350-380°C) and fluid-rock interaction

reservoir.evidence: Permeable, competent coarse clastic zones (wacke, conglomerate) within Ladner Group; up to 30 m thick orebodies

seal.evidence: Barren alteration envelope with K and Ba depletion; overlying siltstone and argillite may provide some seal

timing.evidence: Mineralization between mid-Cretaceous and late Eocene; limited isotopic and fluid inclusion data

preservation.evidence: Deposit preserved at shallow depth (1-3 km); truncated by Richardson fault; some erosion but mineralization subcrops

metallogenic_belt: Manitouwadge greenstone belt, Wawa subprovince, Superior Province
tectonic_setting: Archean greenstone belt, volcanic arc or rifted arc setting, affected by polyphase deformation and upper amphibolite facies metamorphism

source.evidence: Metals sourced from felsic volcanic rocks and synvolcanic trondhjemite; hydrothermal fluids leached metals from volcanic pile.

transport.evidence: Fluids transported via permeable volcaniclastic aquifers and focused along D1 faults and fractures; stockwork zones indicate subsurface conduits.

trap.evidence: Chemical precipitation on seafloor or near-seafloor replacement; iron-formation horizons and alteration zones act as chemical traps; Cu-Zn zoning indicates temperature gradients.

reservoir.evidence: Massive and semimassive sulfide lenses hosted by iron-formation and altered volcanic rocks; stockwork zones in footwall alteration.

seal.evidence: Sillimanite-muscovite-quartz schist and iron-formation may have acted as seals; later deformation and metamorphism modified original permeability.

timing.evidence: U-Pb zircon age of 2720 ± 3 Ma for synvolcanic trondhjemite and felsic volcanism; mineralization is syngenetic with volcanic activity.

preservation.evidence: Deposits preserved despite polyphase deformation and upper amphibolite facies metamorphism; some remobilization but primary features retained.

metallogenic_belt: Al Wajh gold district, Midyan terrane, Arabian-Nubian Shield
tectonic_setting: Neoproterozoic accretionary orogen, Arabian-Nubian Shield

source.evidence: Oxygen isotopes suggest low-grade metamorphic fluid source; gold sourced from mafic volcanic rocks with elevated background Au

transport.evidence: Fluid transport along D2 strike-slip shear zones and D3 thrust faults; multiple deformation episodes facilitated fluid flow

trap.evidence: Gold precipitated in quartz veins within dilatant zones of shear zones and thrust faults; structural traps include fold crests and fault bends

reservoir.evidence: Quartz veins and sheeted zones in altered wall rock; limited extent and small tonnage

seal.evidence: No specific seal identified; veins are open to surface; sericite alteration may have provided some sealing

timing.evidence: Main mineralization during D2 and D3 deformation episodes, constrained between 700-660 Ma; minor D4 veins at ~620 Ma

preservation.evidence: Deposits are small and shallow; ancient mining to only 8 m depth; veins may pinch out at depth; no evidence of deep preservation

metallogenic_belt: Coeur d'Alene mining district, Silver Belt, Belt Supergroup, North American craton
tectonic_setting: Cretaceous accretion and suturing of exotic terranes onto western Idaho, associated with regional compression and formation of granitic batholiths

source.evidence: Metamorphic/formation fluids derived from Belt Supergroup metasedimentary rocks; high 87Sr/86Sr ratios indicate crustal source

transport.evidence: Fluids ascended along steep faults and fractures parallel to reverse faults; high temperature (325°C) and pressure (1-3 kbar) regime

trap.evidence: Siderite precipitation due to temperature increase and CO2 effervescence; U-shaped isotopic trends indicate deposition from vein margins inward

reservoir.evidence: Tabular veins in fractured metasedimentary rocks; sharp contacts with wall rock; vein widths up to 2 m

seal.evidence: No specific seal mentioned; veins are open to later faulting and brecciation

timing.evidence: Sr isotope data indicate Mesozoic or younger age; likely Late Cretaceous to early Tertiary; unsupported radiogenic Sr requires young formation

preservation.evidence: Veins are undeformed except for minor postdepositional faulting; preserved at depths of 3-10 km

metallogenic_belt: Cape Smith fold belt, Ungava trough, Quebec
tectonic_setting: Within-plate environment subject to hot spot volcanism in a distended continental regime, later ocean crust formation

source.evidence: Parental magma derived from ~20% melting of garnet lherzolite; high CaO content (7.21-11.26%)

transport.evidence: PGE transported by silicate magma; later migration of S-bearing liquid and fluid phases

trap.evidence: Sulfide precipitation due to decreasing sulfide capacity from CaO removal and increasing SiO2; R factor 100-1000

reservoir.evidence: Pegmatitic gabbro horizon as host; reef up to 1 m thick, 2 km long

seal.evidence: Overlying anorthosite and gabbro layers; no specific seal discussed

timing.evidence: No absolute ages reported; relative timing from crystallization sequence

preservation.evidence: Intrusion preserved; some hydrothermal alteration and carbonatized zones noted

metallogenic_belt: Kibaran belt, Central African tin belt
tectonic_setting: Mesoproterozoic Kibaran mobile belt, postorogenic extension related to G4 granite magmatism

source.evidence: Highly fractionated, peraluminous G4 granites (tin granites) derived from crustal melting, enriched in Sn, W, Nb, Ta, Li, Cs, Rb, B, F.

transport.evidence: Pegmatite lenses and hydrothermal vein systems along fractures and shear zones; fluid circulation related to granite emplacement.

trap.evidence: Pegmatite lenses with internal zoning; quartz cores surrounded by mineralized zones; hydrothermal veins in metasedimentary rocks.

reservoir.evidence: Pegmatite lenses up to 400 m wide and several km long; quartz-muscovite veins and quartz veins.

seal.evidence: Country rocks (metasediments) provide some seal; later brittle deformation allowed fluid escape.

timing.evidence: U-Pb columbite ages: 962 ± 2 Ma (Kivuvu) and 968+33 Ma (Ruhembe); Pan-African overprint at ca 620 Ma.

preservation.evidence: Deep weathering and Pan-African brittle reworking redistributed Au and Bi; primary pegmatite minerals partially preserved.

metallogenic_belt: New England orogen, Lachlan fold belt, eastern Australia
tectonic_setting: high-level epizonal plutons in a fold belt

source.evidence: Magmatic aqueous phase scavenging metals from larger volumes of granite; elevated Mo, Cu, Au, S, Zn in interconnected miarolitic texture-bearing phases indicate hydrothermal enrichment from magmatic volatiles.

transport.evidence: Interconnected miarolitic texture provides direct evidence of magmatic volatile-phase permeability and connectivity, enabling advection of fluids through spanning clusters.

trap.evidence: Mineralization occurs in leucocratic segregations and apical portions of plutons; precipitation likely due to cooling and decompression, but specific trap mechanism not detailed.

reservoir.evidence: Host rocks are granitic phases with miarolitic cavities and aplitic groundmass; porosity from interconnected miaroles provides space for mineralization.

seal.evidence: No explicit seal described; overlying volcanics and sediments may act as cap, but not discussed.

timing.evidence: Permo-Triassic age inferred from regional geology, but no direct age data for mineralization or interconnected miarolitic texture.

preservation.evidence: Texture preserved in high-level plutons; some erosion has exposed alluvial Sn deposits, indicating partial preservation.

metallogenic_belt: Andean cordillera, southern Peru
tectonic_setting: extensional normal faulting within a Neogene calc-alkaline volcanic belt, related to Quechua 1 tectonism

source.evidence: Lead isotope data and tellurium abundance suggest igneous source for metals; magmatic fluid component inferred from oxygen isotopes

transport.evidence: Hydrothermal fluids focused along normal faults; fluid flow steep, with some horizontal component

trap.evidence: Ore deposited at fault junctions, highly fractured wall rocks, and changes in vein orientation; open-space filling

reservoir.evidence: Veins hosted by early Miocene volcanic rocks; fractures and faults provide permeability

seal.evidence: Barren quartz caps at surface; alluvium covers some veins; postmineral tilting but no major erosion

timing.evidence: K-Ar and 40Ar/39Ar dating of adularia and alunite constrain mineralization at 17.8 ± 0.1 Ma, about 1 m.y. after Sarpane volcanism

metallogenic_belt: Appalachian thrust belt, lower St. Lawrence area

trap.evidence: Fractures and dissolution pores in sandstone served as traps for vein and disseminated mineralization.

reservoir.evidence: Sandstone porosity and permeability provided reservoir space; vein-only deposits have restricted permeability.

seal.evidence: Mudstones may act as seals, but no direct evidence discussed.

timing.evidence: Mineralization age ~450 Ma based on structural studies and model galena Pb ages; Rb-Sr dating of barite gives 490 ± 40 Ma.

metallogenic_belt: Eastern Goldfields province, Yilgarn craton

metallogenic_belt: Quadrilátero Ferrífero, southern São Francisco Craton
